Details: Oxidation of dichlorvos by ozone can be effective depending on the ozone dose, contact time and water quality parameters. As shown in Figure 1, one study showed the removal of dichlorvos with ozone alone, and with ozone combined with hydrogen peroxide. The hydrogen peroxide to ozone ratio remained constant at 0.5 for this study. The pH was 7.3. Ozone with hydrogen peroxide showed more removal for ozone concentrations less than 4.33 mg/L. Transformation products were not reported.
